factors.summary: Demand trends are mixed with ABL segment experiencing a decline in sales while ISG segment shows growth.

evidence.text: Net sales for the first quarter of fiscal 2024 decreased $63.2 million, or 6.3%, to $934.7 million, compared with $997.9 million in the prior-year period due to a decline in sales within our ABL segment, partially offset by higher sales within our ISG segment.
evidence.subsection: First Quarter of Fiscal 2024 Compared with First Quarter of Fiscal 2023

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is decelerating as ABL segment sales decline despite ISG growth.

evidence.text: Net sales for the first quarter of fiscal 2024 decreased $63.2 million, or 6.3%, to $934.7 million, compared with $997.9 million in the prior-year period due to a decline in sales within our ABL segment, partially offset by higher sales within our ISG segment.
evidence.subsection: First Quarter of Fiscal 2024 Compared with First Quarter of Fiscal 2023

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are improving due to favorable material costs and lower operating expenses.

evidence.text: Gross profit margin increased 410 basis points to 45.8% from 41.7% compared with the prior-year period. Operating profit margin increased to 16.4% for ABL and 8.3% for ISG.
evidence.subsection: First Quarter of Fiscal 2024 Compared with First Quarter of Fiscal 2023

factors.summary: The company is able to hold prices, with some support from cost reductions and favorable market conditions.

evidence.text: Gross profit increased compared with the prior period due primarily to favorable material and import costs, partially offset by higher labor, overhead, and quality costs as well as the fall through of the net sales decline.
evidence.subsection: First Quarter of Fiscal 2024 Compared with First Quarter of Fiscal 2023

factors.summary: Input costs are favorable, contributing to improved margins.

evidence.text: Gross profit increased compared with the prior period due primarily to favorable material and import costs, partially offset by higher labor, overhead, and quality costs as well as the fall through of the net sales decline.
evidence.subsection: First Quarter of Fiscal 2024 Compared with First Quarter of Fiscal 2023

factors.summary: Unit volumes are declining in the ABL segment but increasing in ISG, driven by new customers and product mix.

evidence.text: ABL net sales decreased due to lower net sales across all channels except the retail sales channel. ISG net sales increased due to the acquisition of KE2 Therm, price increases, and favorable product mix.
evidence.subsection: First Quarter of Fiscal 2024 Compared with First Quarter of Fiscal 2023

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin products in ISG and lower-margin in ABL, with ISG showing growth but lower margins.

evidence.text: ISG operating profit decreased due to increased employee-related costs and professional fees, partially offset by higher net sales. ABL operating profit increased due to improved profitability on lower sales.
evidence.subsection: First Quarter of Fiscal 2024 Compared with First Quarter of Fiscal 2023

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing, putting pressure on margins.

evidence.text: The decrease in SD&A expenses was due primarily to lower commissions, freight costs, and amortization, partially offset by higher employee-related costs. Operating profit increased due to higher gross profit, partially offset by increased employee-related costs.
evidence.subsection: First Quarter of Fiscal 2024 Compared with First Quarter of Fiscal 2023

factors.summary: Capital expenditures are maintained at a moderate level, signaling confidence in current business needs.

evidence.text: We invested $14.6 million and $18.2 million in property, plant, and equipment during the three months ended November 30, 2023 and 2022, respectively. Investments were primarily in new and enhanced information technology, equipment, and facility improvements.

factors.summary: Shareholder returns are maintained through dividends and share repurchases, signaling management confidence.

evidence.text: We paid dividends of $4.1 million and repurchased 0.3 million shares for $50.0 million during the quarter, maintaining our capital allocation priorities.

factors.summary: Mergers and acquisitions are limited, with only one acquisition in the prior year and no new deals in the current quarter.

evidence.text: There were no acquisitions or divestitures during the first three months of fiscal 2024. We acquired KE2 Therm in May 2023 to expand ISG's product portfolio.

factors.summary: Debt leverage is stable with cash on hand and borrowing capacity sufficient to meet obligations.

evidence.text: Our cash on hand combined with borrowing capacity totaled $1.1 billion, and we were in compliance with all covenants under our financing arrangements.
evidence.subsection: Financial Condition, Capital Resources, and Liquidity

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is stable, supporting capital allocation and operations.

evidence.text: We generated $190.0 million of cash flows from operating activities, maintaining our ability to fund operations and capital priorities.
evidence.subsection: Financial Condition, Capital Resources, and Liquidity

factors.summary: Construction activity is a seasonal factor with historical sales peaks in the last two quarters of the fiscal year.

evidence.text: Our business exhibits some seasonality, with net sales being affected by weather and seasonal demand on construction and installation programs, particularly during the winter months.
